rxn_class_summary: Electrophotocatalytic reductive three-component 1,2-diarylation of alkenes with aryl halides and cyanoaromatics (isocyanopyridines) in an undivided cell, mediated by phenanthrene and 4-DPAIPN photocatalyst.
electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction of phenanthrene mediator generates phenanthrene radical anion, which reduces ArX to the aryl halide radical anion that fragments to aryl radical; anodic oxidation of DABCO/formate provides sacrificial electrons (replacing sacrificial metal anode).
electron_flow: Cathode reduces phenanthrene to its radical anion, which performs outer-sphere SET to ArX, giving an aryl halide radical anion that mesolytically cleaves to aryl radical; the aryl radical adds to the styrene to give a benzylic radical that couples with the photoreduced cyanoaromatic radical anion (formed by SET from PC*– or CO2•–), and decyanation delivers the 1,2-diaryl product. DABCO/formate are oxidized at the anode to regenerate the catalysts.
rate_determining_step: Formation of the aryl halide radical anion via SET from phenanthrene radical anion (TS_I, ΔG‡ = 31.6 kcal/mol in DFT model with iodobenzene)

probe_reagents.name: current on/off control
probe_reagents.observation: No 4aaa formed without electric current; electrocatalysis essential.

probe_reagents.name: light on/off control
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction proceeds without light or 4-DPAIPN but in lower yields; photoredox significantly improves yield.

probe_reagents.name: phenanthrene omission
probe_reagents.observation: Yield drops sharply without phenanthrene, indicating its role as cathodic mediator.

probe_reagents.name: DABCO omission / amine swap (DIEA, Et3N, quinuclidine)
probe_reagents.observation: Without DABCO yield falls to 7%; other amines give 15–58%, indicating DABCO acts as sacrificial electron donor.

probe_reagents.name: HCO2Na omission
probe_reagents.observation: Yield drops to 11%, indicating formate acts as reductant/H source via CO2•–.

probe_reagents.name: NaOAc omission
probe_reagents.observation: Yield drops to 42%, supporting role in halide/cyanide removal.

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of substrates and additives
probe_reagents.observation: 1a Ep/2red = -2.79 V; 2a -2.46 V; 3a -1.97 V; phenanthrene -2.58 V (shifted to -2.29 V with 2a, -2.15 V with 3a); DABCO/HCO2Na/NaOAc/4-DPAIPN mixture shows reduced potential -0.96 V for 2a, demonstrating cooperative reductive activation.

notes: An alternative photoredox-only pathway is also proposed in which reductive quenching of PC* by DABCO yields PC•–, which together with CO2•– (from HCO2Na/DABCO•+) reduces the aryl halide directly; both the electrocatalytic (phenanthrene-mediated) and photoredox pathways operate cooperatively. DFT shows direct dissociation of CN– to pyridyl radical R4 is highly endergonic (+25.6 kcal/mol), ruling out a free pyridyl radical pathway; instead, radical-radical coupling between benzylic radical and cyanoaromatic radical anion followed by decyanation (TS_IV, 7.9 kcal/mol) is favored. Overall process is exergonic by 82.1 kcal/mol.
